ead.archdesc:
======= RMA03832Cornell Concert Commission records37-7-3832Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
November 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
humanThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:42:54 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell Concert Commission records
Cornell Concert Commission
Chodock, Robert,
Kibrick, Howard
37-7-3832
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
4 mapcase folders. 4 mapcase folders.
1971-2017. Newspapers, photocopies, photographs, scrapbooks. Material documenting the shows and operations of the Cornell Concert Commission mostly from 1971-1992, includes tour riders, posters, advertising material, and scrapbooks. Collection material in English 6948619
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
November 2017
EAD encoding:
Kristen Reichenbach, November 2017
Date modified:
Jude Corina, January 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Material documenting the shows and operations of the Cornell Concert Commission mostly from 1971-1992, includes tour riders, posters, advertising material, and scrapbooks.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Concert Files
Series II. Scrapbooks
Series III. Posters
Series IV. Assorted Files
Cite As:
Cornell Concert Commission records #37-7-3832. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Posters. Rock music. Rock concerts. Concerts. Cornell University -- : Students. Grateful Dead (Musical group)
6948619 <<<<<<< HEAD Concert Files
Scope and Contents
Includes tour riders, publicity material, correspondence, financial records, crew lists, newspaper clippings, and show advertismentsAretha Franklin1971-09-1721
Scope and Contents
Tour rider includes clause emphasizing no all white audiencesSha Na Na; Jim Spherris1972-02-2622Jethro Tull; Wild Turkey1972-04-1523Elton John1972-09-2624
Scope and Contents
Includes notes on incidents during showBlood, Sweat & Tears; Charlie Starr1972-10-2025Beach Boys1972-11-1726The Byrds1973-02-1027John McLaughlin1973-02-2328Loggins & Messina1973-03-1129Stevie Wonder1973-03-30210Arlo Gutherie1973-04-14211Seatrain1973-05-07212Deep Purple1973-06-12213Emerson, Lake and Palmer1973-07-13214Fall '73 Concerts1973-09-1973-12215Paul Simon; Urubama; Jesse Dixon Singers1973-10-19216Sly & the Family Stone; the Wailers1973-11-03217Judy Collins1973-11-17218Emerson, Lake and Palmer1973-12-08219
Scope and Contents
Press release regarding show cancellation, mentions financial settlement discussions from Deep Purple showJoni Mitchell1974-02-03220Yes; John Martyn1974-02-24221The Four Tops1974-03-02222
Scope and Contents
CancelledTemptations1974-03-02223Jefferson Starship1974-03-31224Gregg Allman; Cowboy; Boyer & Tarlton1974-04-16225Cornell Folk Festival1974-04-19-1974-04-20226Creative Source; Voices of East Harlem; New Birth1974-05-10227Leon Russell1974-05-11228Martin Mull1974-05-13229The Band1974-09-04230Santana1974-10-14231Taj Mahal with Minnie Ripperton1974-10-26232Taj Mahal with Minnie Ripperton1974-10-26233Cornell Jazz Festival1974-11-16234Billy Joel and Wendy Waldman1974-12-07235Jackson Browne and Phoebe Snow1975-03-15236Jackson Browne and Phoebe Snow1975-03-15237Herbie Hancock1975-04-05238
Scope and Contents
Miles Davis cancelledCornell Folk Festival1975-04-18-1975-04-19239Donald Byrd and the Blackbyrds1975-05-10240National Lampoon Show1975-09-26241One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest1975-10-24242Jerry Garcia Band1975-10-27243Beach Boys1975-11-17244Royal Shakespeare Company1975-11-18245Beach Boys1975-11-27246Melissa Manchester with Eric Andersen1976-04-11247Renaissance1976-04-24248Loggins & Messina1976-05-08249New Riders of the Purple Sage1976-10-31250
Scope and Contents
Tour rider includes suggestion to hire "two ladies" to serve as hostessesSeals & Crofts1976-11-12251David Bromberg Band; Kenny Rankin1977-03-12252The New Commander Cody Band1977-05-06253Grateful Dead1977-05-0812Linda Ronstadt1977-10-27254
Scope and Contents
Includes note about "the ridiculous audience control pattern"Renaissance1977-11-01255Marshall Tucker Band1977-12-11256Emerson, Lake & Palmer1978-01-30257
Scope and Contents
Tour rider includes detailed food requestsPete Seeger1978-04-15258Marshall Tucker Band1978-04-23259Springfest schedule1978-05-04-1978-05-08260Sea Level; Horslips1978-05-05261Meat Loaf1978-05-08262
Scope and Contents
CancelledRobert Palmer; Robert Hunter1978-05-08263Doobie Brothers1978-09-29264
Scope and Contents
Tour rider includes request for 500 lbs. of dry iceRobert Klein; Stanky Brown Group1978-10-05265Patti Smith Group1978-10-14266Bruce Springsteen1978-11-07267Boston; Sammy Hagar1978-11-17268Heart1979-01-28269
Scope and Contents
Tour rider includes request for 350 lbs. of dry ice; emphasis placed on organic foodsThe Kinks1979-02-23270
Scope and Contents
Includes concert t-shirt sketchesSouthside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes1979-03-09271Renaissance1979-04-27272Springfest1979-05-04273
Scope and Contents
Wet Willie; Stanky BrownJoe Jackson; the Records1979-09-14274Cars1979-10-07275Chick Corea and Gary Burton1979-10-19276Laughing Dogs; the Sinceros1979-10-25277Stanley Clarke with friends1979-11-10278
Scope and Contents
CancelledThe Police; Steel Pulse1979-11-30279Cindy Bullens1979-12-07280Steve Forbert1980-02-09281Dave Mason; Hans Olson1980-02-15282The Kinks; Steve Forbert1980-03-02283
Scope and Contents
Includes flyers advertising WVBR's Romeo and Juliet contest to pair up strangers for dinner and the concertWarren Zevon1980-04-11284The Pretenders1980-05-02285The Grateful Dead1980-05-07286
Scope and Contents
Tour rider includes clause refusing to perform before audiences segregated on the basis of race, color, creed or sexB. Willie Smith1980-05-15287Steve Forbert1980-10-02288Al Jarreau1980-10-18289Utopia1980-11-06290George Thorogood; the Destroyers1980-11-08291The Ramones1981-02-28292Pat Metheny1981-03-16293The Grateful Dead1981-05-16294
Scope and Contents
Includes memo detailing injuries from this concert and Kinks showUtopia1981-11-06295Utopia1981-11-06296Allman Brothers1981-12-06297George Thorogood; the Delaware Destroyers1984-12-08298Echo & the Bunnymen; the Leather Nun1988-02-0731Megadeth; Warlock; Sanctuary1988-04-1732
Scope and Contents
Tour rider prohibits "Slam Dancing and Stage Diving".UB40; Untouchables1988-10-1833
Scope and Contents
Student Assembly commends Cornell Concert Commission, claiming "the group stirred up more excitement on campus than any group has in years… you have quieted, for a time, the many students who clamor for big-name bands to come on campus". Enclosed is a form detailing a poster violation filed by the Robert Purcell Union (along with copy of offending poster).Tracy Chapman; John Hammond1988-11-2234
Scope and Contents
Tracy Chapman had "just concluded performing with the Amnesty International Tour all over the world, and [had] scheduled only five shoes in the Western Hemisphere, one of which is at Cornell".Wynton Marsalis1988-12-0335
Scope and Contents
Contract prohibits the presence of a piano "in the artist's suite".Stanley Jordan1989-02-1836
Scope and Contents
Includes ballot for election of Cornell Concert Commission Executive Board.Ladysmith Black Mambazo; Samite1989-03-0637Edie Bricknell & New Bohemians; Steve Forbert1989-04-0938Tito Puente; De La Soul1989-04-1639
Scope and Contents
Concert was co-presented by the Third World Student Programming Board.SpringfestSR-6349, SR-207421989-05-05310
Scope and Contents
Performers: the Toasters; the Feelies; Includes two 7" vinyl records featuring The Toaster's "Talk Is Cheap", and a letter discussing the possibility of hosting a summer venue at Schoelkopf Field.Joe Jackson1989-09-27311
Scope and Contents
Artist specifically agreed to perform, "An Evening With" (see additional provisions in contract).Reggaefest1989-11-10312
Scope and Contents
Performers: Third World; Soul Jahs; bop (harvey)The Alarm; The Kevin McDermott Orchestra1989-12-07313Erasure1990-02-22314
Scope and Contents
Includes letter detailing a theft that took place after the concert, and very detailed advertising routes for Cornell Concert Commission members.Robert Fripp & The League of Crafty Guitarists1990-04-01315
Scope and Contents
Includes letter describing artist's appreciation for Cornell University's excellent hospitality and cetering services, and index cards with reviews of the concertMTV's Save the Earth Campaign1990-04-02-1990-04-06316
Scope and Contents
Includes information regarding the MTV Save the Earth Campaign; stickers from the event, a copy of the April edition of MTV To Go, along with an essay of sorts titled "CIJ on being a woman in the music industry" .Hugh Masekela1990-04-29317Springfest1990-05-04318
Scope and Contents
Performer: NBRQ, CancelledSaturn V Laser Light Concert1990-08-24319Midnight Oil; The Origin1990-09-30320Little Feat; Joe Ely1990-10-14321
Scope and Contents
The Joe Ely tour rider explicitly demands "no insects or animal organs - please!"Yo! MTV Raps1990-10-26322
Scope and Contents
CancelledBranford Marsalis1990-11-11323Indigo Girls1990-12-06324Indigo Girls1990-12-07325Timothy Wright (and the Concert Choir); The Wade Sisters, Lorraine Stancil1991-02-08326Living Colour; Urban Dance Squad1991-02-17327Spyro Gyra; Jay Beckenstein1991-04-14328Boogie Down Productions; Ed O.G.; Da Bulldogs1991-04-19329
Scope and Contents
Commercial thirty seconds in length was broadcasted on MTV and ESPN sometime between 6 AM and midnightGroup Effort1991-04-23330
Scope and Contents
Original date of concert April 23, 1991 was cancelled, reschedule form does not indicate new dateBernie Milton and the Soul Patrol1991-08-23331Jesus Jones; Ned's Atomic Dustbin1991-10-06332Squeeze; Katydids1991-10-27333Crosby & Nash1991-11-17334Vanessa Bell Armstrong; Last Appeal (Cancelled)1992-02-07335
Scope and Contents
Involved collaboration between Cornell Concert Commission and the Festival of Black Gospel Committee; cancelled because record label did not respond in time. Includes resume of William David Stewart, a gospel singer. Enclosed is a letter from the Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority requesting funds to host "The Boy's Choir of Harlem".Naughty by Nature, Queen Latifah, and Blacksheep1992-03-29336
Scope and Contents
The Flavor Unit Tour10,000 Maniacs; The Heartbeats1992-04-10337The Show From Hell (Brash '92)1992-05-03338
Scope and Contents
Performers: Violent Femmes, Fishbone, Blue yster Cult, and Tribe. Transcripts of WJPZ Syracuse radio broadcasts of this event are included.The Show From Hell (Brash '92)1992-05-03339The Show From Hell (Brash '92)1992-05-03340
Scope and Contents
Includes an investigation report detailing two incidents involving minors; "the seventeen year old has a possible broken ankle… [and] the sixteen year old was kicked in the head…"Chick Corea Akoustic Band + One1992-09-25341Scrapbooks1972-1989Scrapbook1972-19741
Scope and Contents
Includes Elton John. Jason, Blood Sweat and Tears, Beach Boys, The Byrds, Herbie Hancock, Miles Davis, Bobby Byrd and the JB's, Mahavishnu Orchestra, Loggins and Messina, Deep Purple has information about the riot during the show.Scrapbook19731
Scope and Contents
Includes various acts, some clippings for concerts from performers who came to Cornell, but not necessarily for their Cornell performance.Scrapbook1973-19741
Scope and Contents
Includes Joni Mitchell, Yes, Four Tops, Jefferson Starship, The Mahavishnu Orchestra and Weather Report, Judy Collins, Paul Simon, Sly and the Family Stone, David Crosby and Graham Nash, The Band with Aztec Two-Step, Santana, Taj Mahal with Minnie Ripperton, Return to Forever with Chick Corea, The Eleventh House with Larry Coryell, Billy Joel.Scrapbook1977-19781
Scope and Contents
Includes Linda Ronstadt, Renaissance, New Riders of the Purple Sage, Grateful Dead.Scrapbook19891
Scope and Contents
Includes Stanley Jordan, Lady Smith Black Mambazo with Samite, Edie Brickell and New Bohemians, Tito Puente and De La Soul.Posters1972-1979, 1985-1989Tom PaxtonMarch 215
Scope and Contents
Poster design is a silk screen copy of a North Vietnamese postage stamp portraying a woman warrior.Pamela PollandDate Unknown2Miles Davis, Herbie HancockDate Unknown2
Scope and Contents
Count: 3Mahavishnu Orchestra, Weather Report2

Scope and Contents
Accounts written in 2016 describe May 8, 1977 concertGrateful Dead photosJon Reis2017-0513
Scope and Contents
Reprint photos of May 8, 1977 concert at Barton Hall ======= Concert Files
